question: Based on the chart, did the first six months of the calendar year (January–June) or the last six months (July–December) have higher total product sales?

options.A: Total sales were higher in January–June.
options.B: Total sales were higher in July–December.
options.C: Both periods had equal total sales.
options.D: The chart does not provide enough information to compare the two periods.

explanation: The months on the x‑axis are arranged in reverse (Dec back to Jan), so the filled area shape is misleading if read left‑to‑right. When you re‑order the months to the natural calendar sequence and sum Jan–Jun versus Jul–Dec, you find that the first half actually has higher total sales. The inappropriate month order can easily invert this interpretation.
